criteria.label: Define generalization and distinguish it from memorizing the training set.
criteria.example: Write one sentence: generalization is low error on unseen (x,y) from the same problem; memorization is high train score that does not transfer to held-out data.

criteria.label: Compute and interpret the generalization gap from train and test error.
criteria.example: Given train acc=0.96 and test acc=0.71, state gap=0.25 and conclude likely overfitting with weak generalization.

criteria.label: Explain why held-out test metrics estimate generalization but training metrics do not.
criteria.example: State that fit() saw training rows so train metrics measure fit; test rows were excluded so predict(X_test) mimics unseen production inputs.

criteria.label: Demonstrate train vs test evaluation without data leakage in a sklearn workflow.
criteria.example: Split data, fit scaler and model on X_train only, report accuracy on X_test — never call fit on X_test or the full X.

criteria.label: Relate generalization failure to overfitting and name one mitigation pointer topic.
criteria.example: Describe low train / high test error as overfitting hurting generalization; point to early stopping or bias–variance for deeper diagnosis.
